evals.prompt: Commit my staged changes. I've added the M3LError hierarchy and M3LResult type in packages/m3l-common/src/core/errors/. These surface through the ./core barrel; the three-entry exports map is unchanged.
evals.expected_output: A git commit with: subject starting with 'feat:' in lowercase imperative tense ≤70 chars; body mentioning the public symbols (M3LError, M3LResult); a semver impact statement noting the exports map is unchanged; a Co-Authored-By footer.
expectations: Subject line starts with 'feat:' in lowercase
Subject line uses imperative present tense (e.g. 'implement' or 'add', not 'implemented' or 'added')
Subject line is 70 characters or fewer
Body mentions at least one public symbol (M3LError, M3LResult, M3LErrorUtils, or similar)
Body includes a semver impact statement (e.g. 'exports map unchanged' or 'no semver break')
Body includes a Co-Authored-By footer with noreply@anthropic.com

evals.prompt: Commit the staged change — I just ran prettier on pnpm-workspace.yaml, nothing else changed.
evals.expected_output: A subject-only commit: 'chore: prettier format workspace file' or similar — no body, since this is a mechanical formatting run.
expectations: Subject line starts with 'chore:' in lowercase
Subject line is 70 characters or fewer
No body is generated (mechanical formatting change needs no explanation)

evals.prompt: Commit this — I edited .github/workflows/claude-pr-review.yml to add a condition that skips the workflow run when the PR author is dependabot[bot].
evals.expected_output: A subject-only commit: 'ci: do not review dependabot PRs' or similar — no scope, no body.
expectations: Subject line starts with 'ci:' in lowercase (no scope like 'ci(workflows):')
Subject line is 70 characters or fewer
No body generated (single-line CI tweak is self-explanatory)

evals.prompt: Commit my staged changes. I renamed M3LPaths.outputDir to M3LPaths.archiveDir everywhere — internal call-sites and the public type. This is a breaking change for any consumer using the old property name.
evals.expected_output: A feat! commit with: 'feat!:' subject naming the renamed symbol; body explaining the reason + which call-sites changed; migration instructions; BREAKING CHANGE footer; Co-Authored-By footer.
expectations: Subject line starts with 'feat!:' (breaking change marker present)
Subject line names the renamed symbol (outputDir or archiveDir)
Body includes migration instructions (what consumers must change)
Body includes a BREAKING CHANGE note (footer or prose)
Body includes a Co-Authored-By footer with noreply@anthropic.com

evals.prompt: Commit this. I updated the post-edit-verify hook so it now runs eslint in the loop (after prettier, before typecheck). I also edited the implement-submodule spoke prompt to front-load contract nuances. No src/, test, or exports-map changes.
evals.expected_output: A chore commit with a body: subject names what changed; body explains why (what problem this solves); semver impact statement; Co-Authored-By footer.
expectations: Subject line starts with 'chore:' in lowercase
Subject line is 70 characters or fewer
A body is present (this chore has a non-obvious why)
Body includes a semver impact statement ('no semver impact', 'zero semver impact', or equivalent)
Body includes a Co-Authored-By footer with noreply@anthropic.com
